protocol.name: waymux_attach_v1
protocol.copyright:
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT OR Apache-2.0
Copyright © 2026 waymux contributors
Mirrors SPEC.md §5. Version 1.

description.summary: embed a waymux session as a wl_surface in the outer compositor

protocol.description:
This protocol is served by a waymux session process on a dedicated per-session socket (the "attach socket"). A client connecting to that socket speaks Wayland protocol and sees the globals defined here advertised by `wl_registry` — no other globals (`wl_compositor`, etc.) are exposed on this connection.
The client hands the session its outer-compositor display fd via `get_surface`; the session then uses that fd to create a `wl_surface` on the outer compositor and ferry frames from its inner composition into it.

interface.name: waymux_attach_v1
interface.description: Represents a single attach-client-to-session relationship. Creating this object doesn't automatically attach; the client must call `get_surface` to supply its outer display fd and receive the `wl_surface` the session will drive.

request.name: get_surface

description.summary: give the session your outer display fd, get back the surface it will drive

request.description:
The `outer_display_fd` argument is passed via SCM_RIGHTS (Wayland handles this automatically for `fd`-typed arguments). The session uses it to open a Wayland client connection to the outer compositor and creates a `wl_surface` there; that same surface object is returned to the client as `id`.
Calling `get_surface` more than once on the same object is a protocol error.

arg.name: id
arg.summary: the surface, on the outer compositor, that the session will drive

arg.name: outer_display_fd
arg.summary: socket fd of the outer compositor's wl_display

request.name: detach
request.description: The session releases the outer display connection, destroys the outer surface, and returns to headless operation. Any further requests on this object result in a protocol error.

event.name: frame

description.summary: a new frame was committed to the outer surface

event.description: Emitted after the session has attached-and-committed a buffer on the outer surface. `serial` is monotonically increasing; it can be used to pair frames with input events the attach client sent. `timestamp_ns` is nanoseconds since UNIX epoch at commit time.

event.name: resized

description.summary: the session changed its logical output size

event.description: Sent when the session is resized. The attach client should update the outer surface's viewport (via `wp_viewporter`) to reflect the new content aspect ratio.

event.name: occluded
event.description: `is_occluded` is 1 when the outer surface has been unmapped or minimized (session pauses frame handoff to preserve idle CPU), 0 when it comes back into view.
